name: Wudu & Bathroom
epithet: Remembrance at the edge of purity
title: Wudu & Bathroom Adhkar: what to say entering and leaving the bathroom and around wudu, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
meta_description: Short du'as for entering and leaving the bathroom, and the words to say before and after wudu, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source.
keywords: bathroom dua, wudu dua, dua entering bathroom, dua leaving bathroom, dua before wudu, dua after wudu, Bismillah wudu, ablution dhikr, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Some of the smallest moments of the day are the ones we pass through without a thought: stepping into the bathroom, turning on the tap, finishing a wash. The Prophet ﷺ filled even these with the Name of Allah, so that no part of the day was lived heedless of Him.
These are the words for the threshold of the bathroom and for the act of wudu. A few syllables, but they turn an ordinary routine into worship and ask for shelter where the heart is most exposed.
intro: Say the entering du'a before you step in, and the leaving du'a once you are out. Say Bismillah as you begin to wash, and the words of witness once your wudu is complete. The bathroom du'as are said outside the bathroom itself, or silently within the heart while inside.
closing: Wudu is not only washing; it is the light a believer carries to the prayer, and the sunnah is that minor sins fall away with the water. To begin it with His Name and seal it with the testimony of faith is to make the whole act count twice.
O Allah, make us among those who are purified, and let no corner of our day be empty of Your remembrance.

faq.q: Do I recite the bathroom du'as inside the bathroom?
faq.a: No. Say the entering du'a just before you step in, and the leaving du'a as soon as you come out. Inside, the Name of Allah is not spoken aloud, so these words belong at the door, not within.

faq.q: What do I say before wudu?
faq.a: Begin with Bismillah (In the Name of Allah) as you start to wash. When you have finished, say the testimony of faith, and the sunnah du'a asking to be made among those who repent and those who are purified.

faq.q: Why say a du'a for something as ordinary as wudu?
faq.a: Because the Prophet ﷺ taught us that nothing is too ordinary for the remembrance of Allah. Beginning wudu with His Name and ending it with the shahadah turns a daily routine into an act of worship that lifts the soul along with the body.

email.subject: The words for the smallest moments of your day
email.alt_subject: What to say around wudu and the bathroom
email.preview: Short du'as for the bathroom and for wudu, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

We pass through some moments of the day without a second thought: stepping into the bathroom, turning on the tap, finishing a wash. The Prophet ﷺ filled even these with the Name of Allah, so that no part of his day was lived forgetful of Him.

We have gathered the short du'as for the threshold of the bathroom and for the act of wudu, each with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source. They are only a few words, but they turn an ordinary routine into worship and ask for shelter where the heart is most exposed.

Begin wudu with His Name, seal it with the testimony of faith, and let the light of it carry you to the prayer:

social.hook:
There is a du'a for stepping into the bathroom.

The Prophet ﷺ left no part of the day empty of Allah's Name.
social.caption:
The adhkar of the bathroom and of wudu, the words for the smallest moments of your day.

A du'a for entering, a du'a for leaving, the Name of Allah to begin your wash, and the testimony of faith to seal it. Wudu is the light a believer carries to the prayer, and beginning it this way makes the whole act count twice.

Save this, and read the full set with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/wudu-bathroom

#WuduDua #BathroomDua #Ablution #Dhikr #Dua #HisnAlMuslim #SunnahOfTheProphet #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

10.title: Entering the bathroom
10.note: Said before you step in. The Prophet ﷺ sought refuge in Allah from harm at the very threshold, knowing these are places where the soul is exposed.

11.title: Leaving the bathroom
11.note: A single word, Ghufranaka, asking forgiveness. A beautiful sunnah: having been relieved of harm, the first thing on the tongue is a plea for pardon.

12.title: Before wudu
12.note: Begin your wash with the Name of Allah, and the whole act becomes worship.

13.title: The testimony after wudu
13.note: The Prophet ﷺ said that whoever completes his wudu well and then says this will have the eight gates of Paradise opened for him, to enter by whichever he wishes.

14.title: Among the repentant and the purified
14.note: A second sunnah du'a after wudu, asking to be counted with those who turn back to Allah and those whom He keeps clean.

15.title: Glory and praise after wudu
15.note: The words of tasbih and seeking forgiveness, reported as a seal upon the wudu, written and kept until the Day of Resurrection.
